app.name: Shannon
welcome.skipAria: Skip welcome and start using Shannon
welcome.task.title: What will you use Shannon for?
welcome.task.subtitle: Pick a starting point. You can change anything later.
welcome.task.code.label: Code
welcome.task.code.blurb: Build apps, write scripts, debug and refactor.
welcome.task.writing.label: Writing
welcome.task.writing.blurb: Draft docs, articles, posts, and emails.
welcome.task.research.label: Research
welcome.task.general.label: General
welcome.task.general.blurb: A bit of everything — start here if unsure.
welcome.model.title: Choose your AI provider
welcome.model.subtitle.default: You can change this any time in Settings → Models.
welcome.model.apiKey.label: API key
welcome.model.apiKey.help: Stored locally via Shannon's config. Get a key from your provider's dashboard.
welcome.model.ollama.desc: Local models, no API key needed
welcome.tools.title: Pick your tools
welcome.tools.subtitle: Recommended for your task. Toggle off anything you don't need — you can change this in Extensions.
welcome.tools.filesystem.label: Filesystem
welcome.tools.filesystem.desc: Read and write files in your workspace.
welcome.tools.git.label: Git
welcome.tools.playwright.label: Playwright
welcome.tools.playwright.desc: Automate the browser for end-to-end tests.
welcome.tools.webSearch.label: Web Search
welcome.tools.webSearch.desc: Look up current information on the web.
welcome.tools.tavily.label: Tavily Research
welcome.tools.workingDir.help: Need a different working directory? Adjust it in {link}.
welcome.done.title: You're all set
welcome.done.subtitle: Here are the keys to know. You can change anything in Settings.
welcome.done.setup.label: Your setup
welcome.done.workingDir.label: Working directory
welcome.done.shortcuts.label: Shortcuts
welcome.done.shortcuts.help: Press {key} any time for the full list.
welcome.done.devMode.title: Show advanced features
welcome.done.devMode.desc: Reveals the Schedules, Triggers, Permission Modes, and Extensions sections in the sidebar. You can toggle this later.
welcome.skills.title: Documents capabilities
welcome.skills.subtitle: Optional skills that let Shannon generate, export, and polish documents via host-installed tools (pandoc, python-docx).
welcome.skills.pandoc.label: Pandoc Documents
welcome.skills.pandoc.desc: Convert Markdown ↔ DOCX/PDF/HTML via pandoc. Requires pandoc installed on your system.
welcome.skills.pydocx.label: Python DOCX Generator
welcome.skills.pydocx.desc: Generate styled .docx files from structured prompts via python-docx. Requires Python 3.10+.
welcome.skills.beautify.label: Markdown Beautify
welcome.skills.beautify.desc: Polish messy Markdown — headings, tables, code blocks, links — into a clean, readable layout.
welcome.skills.later: Skip for now — you can install later from {link}.
welcome.toast.workingDir.failed: Could not save working directory — restart Shannon with --working-dir to apply
welcome.toast.provider.failed: Failed to configure provider. You can finish setup in Settings.
welcome.envDetected.toast: {provider} detected — API key loaded from your environment.
welcome.envDetected.ollama: Ollama detected — no API key required.
welcome.testConnection.invalidKey: Invalid API key — check the key from your provider dashboard
welcome.testConnection.rateLimited: Rate limited — wait a moment and try again
welcome.testConnection.networkUnreachabl: Can't reach {provider} — check your internet connection
welcome.testConnection.failed: Test failed — check the developer console for details
settings.language.label: Language
settings.language.help: Choose the interface language. Changes apply immediately.
settings.notifications.label: Notifications
settings.notifications.help: Fire a native OS notification to verify the renderer is wired up. Full event-driven firing (query complete, errors, agent idle) lands in a future release.
settings.notifications.testBody: If you can see this, native notifications are working.
nav.resize.title: Drag to resize sidebar
nav.simpleMode.title: Simple mode — core navigation. Click for Advanced mode.
nav.devMode.title: Advanced mode — all features visible. Click for Simple mode.
settings.notifications.title: Notifications
settings.notifications.subtitle: Configure webhook delivery for desktop notifications. Settings are written to your Shannon config file.
settings.notifications.customBodyHint: Use {title}, {body}, and {level} placeholders. Output is sent as raw JSON.
settings.notifications.secretHint: Sends an X-Shannon-Signature header (HMAC-SHA256) for custom reverse-proxy receivers. Native Slack/Discord/Feishu/WeChat bots ignore unknown headers.
settings.notifications.clearDescription: Remove the webhook URL and reset all fields. You'll need to reconfigure to resume notifications.
settings.notifications.saved: Webhook configuration saved. Restart the app to apply.
settings.notifications.restartHint: Webhook delivery is wired up at app startup — restart Shannon after changing this config for it to take effect.
settings.notifications.inbound.title: Inbound Messages (Slack + Telegram)
settings.notifications.inbound.subtitle: Configure bot tokens so Slack and Telegram messages can trigger Shannon. Matching messages are emitted to the app; a chat trigger can be wired on top.
settings.notifications.inbound.phase2Not: Listener workers (Slack Socket Mode + Telegram long-poll) auto-start when this config is saved. Matching messages emit an inbound-message event.
settings.notifications.inbound.phase1Not: Phase 1 stores credentials only — no listener is active yet. Phase 2 will wire a polling worker that triggers chats from matching messages.
settings.notifications.outbound.title: Outbound Messages (Slack + Telegram)
settings.notifications.outbound.subtitle: Send messages from Shannon to Slack channels or Telegram chats. Configure credentials per provider; leave a provider blank to skip it.
settings.notifications.outbound.error.em: Configure at least one provider before saving.
settings.notifications.outbound.error.no: No outbound providers configured. Save credentials first.
settings.notifications.outbound.test.all: Test message delivered to all configured providers.
settings.notifications.outbound.test.par: Test message delivered to some providers; see details.
settings.notifications.outbound.test.all: All outbound deliveries failed; see details.
settings.notifications.error.urlInvalid: Webhook URL is not a valid URL.
settings.notifications.error.urlBadSchem: Webhook URL must use http:// or https://.
settings.notifications.error.urlPrivate: Webhook URL cannot point to localhost or a private network range.
settings.notifications.webhook.title: Webhook Notifications
settings.notifications.webhook.subtitle: Configure outbound webhook delivery for desktop notifications.
settings.notifications.wizard.slack.step: Create a new Slack app and configure it for Socket Mode.
settings.notifications.wizard.slack.scop: Copy these scopes when configuring your Slack app bot permissions:
settings.notifications.wizard.slack.mani: Copy this complete manifest JSON and paste it into your Slack app configuration.
settings.notifications.wizard.slack.step: Enable Socket Mode and generate tokens.
settings.notifications.wizard.slack.botT: Found in OAuth & Permissions → Bot User OAuth Token
settings.notifications.wizard.slack.step: Set trigger words and allowed channels for inbound messages.
Save to start the Slack Socket Mode listener. Matching messages will trigger Shannon chats.
settings.notifications.wizard.telegram.s: Use BotFather to create a new bot and get your token.
Paste the bot token from BotFather.
Set trigger words and allowed chats for inbound messages.
settings.notifications.wizard.telegram.c: Chat ID captured from latest message
settings.notifications.wizard.telegram.s: Send a message to your bot, then click 'Capture Chat ID' to auto-detect it.
settings.notifications.wizard.email.desc: Configure email inbox monitoring (Phase 1: credentials only).
settings.notifications.wizard.email.phas: Phase 1 stores credentials only — listener not yet implemented.
footer.tokens: {count, plural, =1 {{count, number} token} other {{count, number} tokens}}

tasks.scheduleForm.parseError: Could not parse. Try "every 15 minutes", "weekdays at 9am", "daily at 09:30", "monthly on day 1 at 8am".
tasks.scheduleForm.webhookInfo: On save, a webhook URL + signing secret will be generated. Trigger via POST with HMAC-SHA256 signature header.
tasks.scheduleForm.eventInfo: Event-driven triggers fire when another routine emits a matching event. Configure event subscriptions after creation in the routine detail view.
tasks.tasksHeader.title: Scheduled Tasks
tasks.tasksHeader.subtitle: Manage and monitor your automated intelligence workflows.
tasks.agentMessagesPanel.title: Agent Messages
tasks.agentMessagesPanel.contentPlacehol: Message content (text only — structured/protocol come from real agents)

tasks.agentMessagesPanel.emptyAll: No inter-agent messages recorded yet. Use "Record test message" above, or wait for real team agents to communicate.
tasks.worktreePanel.title: Task Worktrees
tasks.worktreePanel.subtitle: Spin up isolated git worktrees for unattended routine execution.
tasks.worktreePanel.emptyDesc: Create one above to enable isolated, unattended execution of a scheduled routine.

tasks.hookRoutineCreateDialog.commandHin: Shell command to run when the hook fires.

extensions.mcp.uploadDesc: Install an MCP server from a .mcpb ZIP archive on disk.

extensions.mcp.tier3Note: Use the manual form below for Tier-3 stdio servers, or Featured tab for OAuth.

settings.billing.title: Usage & Billing
settings.billing.subtitle: Manage your subscription plans, view usage metrics, and update payment information.
settings.billing.demoDesc: Billing integration is not yet connected. Plan, cost, and history figures shown here are illustrative sample data, not real usage.
settings.billing.noCostData: No cost data yet. Usage data will appear here once available.
settings.billing.enterpriseHelp: Need to scale further? Contact our
settings.billing.cancelDesc: Are you sure you want to cancel? This will downgrade you to the free tier at the end of your billing period.
settings.billing.legalDesc1: Shannon Desktop processes all data locally. No conversation content is sent to third parties beyond your configured LLM provider.
settings.billing.legalDesc2: Usage telemetry is optional and can be disabled in Advanced Settings.
settings.advanced.title: Advanced Settings
settings.advanced.subtitle: Configure underlying engine parameters and data sovereignty protocols.
settings.advanced.memoryDesc: Manage how the AI persists context and session artifacts over time.
settings.advanced.memoryHelp: Allow agent to reference past conversations.
settings.advanced.privacyDesc: Control your cryptographic signatures and usage telemetry protocols.
settings.advanced.reportingHelp: Share diagnostic data to improve models.
settings.advanced.devDesc: Advanced tools for debugging agent behaviors and observing raw kernel output.
settings.advanced.resetDesc: Resetting to factory settings will permanently delete all local agents, conversation history, and fine-tuning parameters. This action cannot be undone.
settings.advanced.logsDesc2: System logs are available in the Tauri console.
settings.advanced.logsDesc3: Run with RUST_LOG=debug for verbose output.
settings.advanced.keysDesc: API keys are managed through your provider's configuration. Update your key in Model Settings.
settings.advanced.clearDesc: This will clear all cached session data. Active sessions will not be affected.
settings.advanced.resetConfirm: This will permanently delete all local agents, conversation history, and configuration. This cannot be undone.
